---
applyTo: '**'
---

# Internationalisation (i18n) Rules

## Supported locales

This project supports exactly **two locales**:

| Locale code | Language | Direction |
| ----------- | -------- | --------- |
| `ar`        | Arabic   | RTL       |
| `en`        | English  | LTR       |

Both locales are **required** at the content level. Neither is optional.

## Locale type

```ts
// src/lib/i18n/locales.ts
export const LOCALES = ['ar', 'en'] as const;
export type Locale = (typeof LOCALES)[number];
export const DEFAULT_LOCALE: Locale = 'ar';
```

## LocalizedText

All user-visible text that must appear in both languages uses `LocalizedText`:

```ts
type LocalizedText = {
  ar: string;
  en: string;
};
```

Use it for: `title`, `subtitle`, `description`, `shortDescription`, `altText`, etc.
Do **not** use it for: `slug`, `id`, `status`, `category`, `placeType`, `phone`, `mapUrl`.

## Accessing localised text

Use the locale helper to resolve a `LocalizedText` to a single string:

```ts
// src/lib/i18n/helpers.ts
import type { LocalizedText } from '@/types';
import type { Locale } from '@/lib/i18n/locales';

export function localise(text: LocalizedText, locale: Locale): string {
  return text[locale] ?? text['en'] ?? '';
}
```

## Locale in components

- Locale is derived from the URL, user preference, or a client-side state
- Pass `locale` as a prop into components that render localised content
- Do **not** access locale from a global singleton inside render functions
- The `LocaleSwitcher` component is a **client component** (`'use client'`)

## RTL layout

- When `locale === 'ar'`, set `dir="rtl"` on the root `<html>` element
- The root layout must conditionally set the `lang` and `dir` attributes
- Use CSS logical properties (`margin-inline-start`, `padding-inline-end`) instead of `left`/`right` for layout spacing
- Mirror icons and directional arrows for RTL when applicable

```tsx
// src/app/layout.tsx (example)
<html lang={locale} dir={locale === 'ar' ? 'rtl' : 'ltr'}>
```

## SEO metadata localisation

- Every page must provide `<title>` and `<meta name="description">` in the active locale
- Use `LocalizedSeo` and the helper in `src/lib/seo/metadata.ts`
- Provide `hreflang` alternate links for both locales

## Content localisation completeness

- Both `ar` and `en` fields are mandatory in all `LocalizedText` objects
- If a translation is missing during development, use `// TODO: translate` in the near-by comment
- Do **not** silently fall back to English in production — return an error state or flag the incomplete content

## Locale-aware URL strategy

The locale is part of the URL path:

```
/ar/places
/en/places
/ar/places/al-shouf-cedar
/en/places/al-shouf-cedar
```

The `[locale]` segment is the top-level segment under `app/`.

## Dictionary / translation files

Static UI strings (button labels, nav items, empty states, etc.) live in:

```
src/lib/i18n/
  dictionaries/
    ar.ts
    en.ts
  index.ts
```

Dynamic content strings come from Firestore `LocalizedText` fields — not from dictionary files.
